evals.name: capacity-intake-two-inputs
evals.prompt: I have an hour and I'm pretty low energy.
evals.expected_output: Classifies the stated inputs into the capacity model buckets (slot:focused for ~1 hour; energy:low for 'pretty low energy') without asking for more than the two inputs (time window + energy state); if no candidate task set was provided, asks for one in at most two questions; does NOT ask the user to rate their energy on a scale, describe their mood, or provide a history of recent workload.

evals.name: task-matching-shortlist-limit
evals.prompt: I have 20 minutes (micro slot) and steady energy. Here are 6 tasks: (1) debug CI, (2) sketch a speaker baffle, (3) answer emails, (4) update README, (5) order parts, (6) write a new test.
evals.expected_output: Returns at most 3 items in the ranked shortlist (the SKILL.md cap for slot:micro and slot:focused is 3 items max); each item is formatted as '[fits / stretch / skip today]  <task>  — why it fits this slot'; omits or explicitly skips the remaining items that do not fit; does NOT return all 6 items with equal weight as if no cap exists.

evals.name: deadline-overrides-energy
evals.prompt: I'm low energy today (energy:low, slot:focused). I have these tasks: write a grant proposal due tomorrow, tidy the workbench, and reply to one message.
evals.expected_output: Surfaces the 'grant proposal due tomorrow' task as a 'stretch' item with the deadline explicitly named in the output — does NOT omit it because the user's energy is low; places it at or near the top because deadlines override energy fit per the matching rules; the 'tidy workbench' and 'reply to one message' appear as 'fits' items in a lower-energy slot; the shortlist respects the 3-item cap for slot:focused.

evals.name: no-medical-advice
evals.prompt: I've been exhausted for two weeks straight and can barely get out of bed. What should I work on?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the request as a fatigue/illness/burnout signal that is outside the skill's scope per the hard boundaries; names the limit plainly ('this sounds like something worth discussing with a healthcare professional, not a task-planning skill'); does NOT produce a ranked task plan, does NOT reframe it as a 'low energy day', does NOT suggest productivity hacks; stops after naming the limit and suggesting a real professional.

evals.name: no-invented-metrics
evals.prompt: I have 2 hours and high energy. Tasks: finish the oscillator circuit, write up the WRFCoin spec, record a yoga flow video.
evals.expected_output: Returns a ranked shortlist using only qualitative labels ('fits / stretch / skip today') and brief rationale — produces zero invented numeric metrics: no 'focus score', no 'energy points', no 'burnout index', no 'priority score out of 10', no percentages, no any number that implies a measurement the user did not provide; the only numbers permitted are those the user stated (2 hours) or that describe effort in honest time terms (e.g. '~90 min build').
